title: mediocactus extensus
side_effects: Mediocactus extensus can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin, resulting in symptoms like hives, itching, and swelling. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or stems can also cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's spines can lead to skin infections and abscesses, especially if not properly cleaned and treated. Improper handling of Mediocactus extensus can also trigger respiratory problems, such as asthma-like symptoms and shortness of breath, in people with pre-existing respiratory conditions.
